title: Translations. -Friend And Foe. (From Schiller.)
content:
In these epigrams I have altered the form, which in the original is the elegiac distich.



    Dear is my friend, but my foe too
     Is friendly to my good;
    My friend the thing shows I can do,
     My foe, the thing I should.
meaning:
This evocative piece by George MacDonald, titled "Translations. -Friend And Foe. (From Schiller.)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
